content.problemStatement:
When Chef was born, his parents took him to the famous monk Doctor Strange to know whether he will land himself in heaven after his life or not. According to Strange, Chef will live for LLL years in total. If he wants to go to heaven, he must spend at least 50%50\%50% of his life years doing good deeds. He also shows them his future using a string SSS of length LLL where Si=0S_i = 0Si​=0 means the iii-th year will be counted as bad as per the rule books of heaven and Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 means the iii-th year will be counted as good.

Also, Strange can use his special powers to make Chef end his life earlier than that planned by god, i.e, he can choose some L′L'L′ (1≤L′≤L1\leq L'\leq L1≤L′≤L) and make him live for only L′L' L′ years. Strange wants Chef to succeed, so if there is any choice of L′L'L′ that allows Chef to go to heaven, he will do so.

Tell whether Chef can go to heaven.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line contains a single integer LLL.
The second line contains a string SSS of length LLL, consisting of symbols `0` and `1`.
###Output
For each test case, output the answer in a single line: "YES" if Chef can go to heaven and "NO" if not (without quotes).

You may print each character of each string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).

###Constraints


1≤L≤1051 \leq L \leq 10^51≤L≤105
The sum of LLL over all tests does not exceed 10610^6106
###Subtasks
constraints: 1 ≤ T ≤ 10^5 (sum of L over all test cases ≤ 10^6)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer L (1 ≤ L ≤ 10^5), the length of the string. The second line contains a binary string S of length L consisting of characters '0' and '1'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if there exists a prefix length L' (1 ≤ L' ≤ L) such that the number of '1's in the first L' characters is at least 50% of L', otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Prefix of length 2 has 1 good year, 1*2 >= 2, so YES.\nTest case 2: No prefix satisfies the 50% rule, so NO.\nTest case 3: Prefix of length 2 already has 1 good year (50%), so YES.

hidden.label: all bad except first two years

solution.approach: For each test case iterate over the string from left to right, maintaining a counter of good years (number of '1's) seen so far. At position i (1‑based) we have examined a prefix of length i. Chef can go to heaven if 2 * good >= i (i.e., good years are at least 50% of the prefix). As soon as this condition holds for any i we can answer YES; if the loop finishes without satisfying it, answer NO. This is O(L) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the length of the string. Over all test cases the total time is O(ΣL) ≤ 10^6.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    t = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        L = int(next(it))
        s = next(it).strip()
        good = 0
        ok = False
        for i,ch in enumerate(s,1):
            if ch == '1':
                good += 1
            if 2*good >= i:
                ok = True
                break
        out.append('YES' if ok else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
